AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTHWEST OF ROCKY MOUNT, NC ON APRIL 19TH, 2019. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 11 MINUTES AND 12.5 MILES PRODUCING INTERMITTENT TREE AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ACROSS PORTIONS OF NASH COUNTY. THE WORST DAMAGE OCCURRED AS THE TORNADO PASSED OVER NC-4 WHERE ITS MAX WIDTH PEAKED TO 350 YARDS AND SEVERAL FARM STRUCTURES AND A RESIDENCE SUSTAINED EF-1 RATED DAMAGE. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTH OF THE HALIFAX COUNTY BORDER WITH DEBRIS CONTINUING TO BE DEPOSITED FOR SEVERAL ADDITIONAL MILES NORTH OF WHERE THE DAMAGE SWATH ENDED.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SW OF WELDON NC ON APRIL 19TH, 2019. THE TORNADO REMAINED ON THE GROUND FOR FOUR MINUTES, OR JUST OVER 5 MILES, PRODUCING INTERMITTENT TREE AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, PRIMARILY AS IT PASSED THROUGH THE CITIES OF SOUTH WELDON AND WELDON. THE STORM REMAINED ON THE GROUND AS IT CROSSED THE ROANOKE RIVER INTO NORTHAMPTON COUNTY.

ONGOING NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS A SWATH OF TORNADO DAMAGE IMPACTING PORTIONS OF SOUTH WELDON AND WELDON, NC, WITH THE DAMAGE PATH CONTINUING NORTHEASTWARD INTO NORTHAMPTON COUNTY. PRIMARY DAMAGE INDICATORS ARE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES ALONG WITH MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGES. SURVEY WILL BE CONTINUED IN THE NEAR FUTURE. TIME INDICATES THE END TIME OF THE DAMAGE PATH IN THE CITY OF WELDON. UNKNOWN INTENSITY AND DURATION AT THIS TIME.
